introSection:
Wipeout is a steel shuttle roller coaster located at Pleasurewood Hills in Lowestoft, Suffolk, England, UK.

It was one of four Boomerang coasters built in the UK and the only one left in the country.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The roller coaster first operated at the Glasgow Garden Festival in Scotland, UK during 1988. It was called Coca-Cola Roller. The following year, it opened at American Adventure Theme Park in Derbyshire, England, UK and was called Missile. It closed in 2004.

It reopened at Pleasurewood Hills on 28 June 2007.

The ride added new trains from Sunkid Heege in 2025.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: Wipeout currently has light blue track and dark blue supports. It was painted entirely white when operating in Glasgow, keeping that look on its initial relocation to American Adventure, before being repainted grey with blue supports in the mid-1990s.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The original train from Arrow Dynamics was replaced with a Vekoma train from Speed of Sound at Walibi Holland in the Netherlands as part of the move to Pleasurewood Hills.

fullText:
Wipeout is a steel shuttle roller coaster located at Pleasurewood Hills in Lowestoft, Suffolk, England, UK.

It was one of four Boomerang coasters built in the UK and the only one left in the country.

The roller coaster first operated at the Glasgow Garden Festival in Scotland, UK during 1988. It was called Coca-Cola Roller. The following year, it opened at American Adventure Theme Park in Derbyshire, England, UK and was called Missile. It closed in 2004.

It reopened at Pleasurewood Hills on 28 June 2007.

The ride added new trains from Sunkid Heege in 2025.



Wipeout currently has light blue track and dark blue supports. It was painted entirely white when operating in Glasgow, keeping that look on its initial relocation to American Adventure, before being repainted grey with blue supports in the mid-1990s.

The original train from Arrow Dynamics was replaced with a Vekoma train from Speed of Sound at Walibi Holland in the Netherlands as part of the move to Pleasurewood Hills.
